examples.title: Insert into Max-Heap
examples.description: Algorithm to insert a new element into a max-heap using sift-up operation
examples.code:
PROCEDURE Insert(heap[], value)
BEGIN
    heapSize ← heapSize + 1
    heap[heapSize] ← value      // Add at end
    i ← heapSize                // Start from last index
    
    WHILE i > 0 DO
        parent ← (i - 1) / 2    // Parent index
        IF heap[parent] < heap[i] THEN
            SWAP heap[parent], heap[i]
            i ← parent         // Continue up the tree
        ELSE
            BREAK              // Heap property satisfied
        END IF
    END WHILE
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: Insert places new element at the end of array (maintaining complete tree), then repeatedly compares with parent and swaps if parent is smaller. This 'sifts up' the element until heap property is restored. Worst case: O(log n) when element rises to root.

examples.title: Extract-Max from Max-Heap
examples.description: Algorithm to remove and return the maximum element from a max-heap using heapify
examples.code:
PROCEDURE ExtractMax(heap[])
BEGIN
    IF heapSize = 0 THEN
        RETURN NULL           // Empty heap
    END IF
    
    max ← heap[0]              // Store maximum (root)
    heap[0] ← heap[heapSize]   // Move last element to root
    heapSize ← heapSize - 1   // Reduce heap size
    
    Heapify(heap, 0)           // Restore heap property
    RETURN max
END PROCEDURE

PROCEDURE Heapify(heap[], i)
BEGIN
    largest ← i
    left ← 2*i + 1
    right ← 2*i + 2
    
    IF left < heapSize AND heap[left] > heap[largest] THEN
        largest ← left
    END IF
    
    IF right < heapSize AND heap[right] > heap[largest] THEN
        largest ← right
    END IF
    
    IF largest ≠ i THEN
        SWAP heap[i], heap[largest]
        Heapify(heap, largest) // Recursively heapify
    END IF
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: Extract-Max removes root, places last element at root, then calls Heapify to 'sift down' the element by comparing with children and swapping with the larger child until heap property is restored. Time: O(log n)
examples.spaceComplexity: O(log n) for recursive, O(1) for iterative

examples.title: Build Heap from Unsorted Array
examples.description: Python implementation to build a max-heap from unsorted array
examples.code:
def build_max_heap(arr):
    n = len(arr)
    
    # Start from last non-leaf node and heapify each
    # Last non-leaf node is at index (n//2 - 1)
    for i in range(n // 2 - 1, -1, -1):
        heapify(arr, n, i)
    
    return arr

def heapify(arr, n, i):
    largest = i
    left = 2 * i + 1
    right = 2 * i + 2
    
    # Check if left child exists and is greater
    if left < n and arr[left] > arr[largest]:
        largest = left
    
    # Check if right child exists and is greater
    if right < n and arr[right] > arr[largest]:
        largest = right
    
    # Swap and continue heapifying if root is not largest
    if largest != i:
        arr[i], arr[largest] = arr[largest], arr[i]
        heapify(arr, n, largest)

# Example usage:
arr = [4, 10, 3, 5, 1]
print("Original:", arr)
build_max_heap(arr)
print("Max-Heap:", arr)  # Output: [10, 5, 3, 4, 1]
examples.explanation: Build-Heap iterates from the last non-leaf node (index n//2 - 1) up to root, calling heapify on each. This ensures all subtrees satisfy heap property. Time complexity is O(n) despite n/2 calls to heapify because most nodes are near the bottom with smaller heights.

examples.title: Heap Sort Algorithm
examples.description: Complete heap sort implementation using max-heap
examples.code:
def heap_sort(arr):
    n = len(arr)
    
    # Build max-heap
    for i in range(n // 2 - 1, -1, -1):
        heapify(arr, n, i)
    
    # Extract elements from heap one by one
    for i in range(n - 1, 0, -1):
        # Move current root (max) to end
        arr[0], arr[i] = arr[i], arr[0]
        
        # Call heapify on reduced heap
        heapify(arr, i, 0)
    
    return arr

def heapify(arr, n, i):
    largest = i
    left = 2 * i + 1
    right = 2 * i + 2
    
    if left < n and arr[left] > arr[largest]:
        largest = left
    if right < n and arr[right] > arr[largest]:
        largest = right
    if largest != i:
        arr[i], arr[largest] = arr[largest], arr[i]
        heapify(arr, n, largest)

# Example:
arr = [12, 11, 13, 5, 6, 7]
print("Original:", arr)
heap_sort(arr)
print("Sorted:", arr)  # Output: [5, 6, 7, 11, 12, 13]
examples.explanation: Heap Sort works in two phases: 1) Build max-heap O(n), 2) Repeatedly extract max O(n log n). Total: O(n log n). Unlike quicksort, this is guaranteed for all inputs. Space: O(1) in-place.
